en: Applicability to external workplaces, construction sites, and off-site work under the Employee Protection Act must be addressed in written safety documentation.\nExplosives Act, Section 22
Documented blasting competency must support eligibility for an explosives permit.\nExplosives Act, Section 24(2)
Secure storage provisions must be in place to support safe and authorized explosives possession.\nExplosives Act, Section 24(1)
A designated explosives officer must be appointed when explosives are acquired, possessed, imported, transferred, or stored.\nExplosives Act, Section 26(1)
Unusable or defective explosives must be disposed of or destroyed using permitted methods and manufacturer or dealer instructions.\nExplosives Act, Section 9(1-3)
Explosives directories must record acquisition, transfer, use, and destruction by storage location and be retained for 10 years.\nExplosives Act, Section 33(1),(4)
